instructions: Refactor `ContextTarget.provider_name` to remove the hardcoded `Literal` of provider strings. Replace it with a standard `str` type to adhere to the Open/Closed Principle, allowing new providers to be registered without modifying the core model. Ensure any relevant tests in `tests/models/test_core.py` are updated to reflect this change.
anti_patterns: Do NOT import anything from `jitsu.providers`, `jitsu.server`, or `jitsu.cli` into `jitsu.models` to maintain strict Layer 1 isolation.
Do NOT add `# noqa` or `# pragma` markers.
Do NOT remove `ConfigDict(frozen=True)` from the models.
completion_criteria: The `ContextTarget.provider_name` field no longer uses a hardcoded `Literal` of all providers.
Models remain strictly decoupled from other layers.

instructions: Remove LLM prompt engineering instructions from the `description` fields of Pydantic models in Layer 1. Specifically, update `AgentDirective.verification_commands` to solely describe the data type (e.g., 'Commands to verify the phase is complete.') and remove the hardcoded rule prompting.
anti_patterns: Do NOT change the underlying data types, generic type structures, or default values of the models.
Do NOT introduce any external dependencies for validation.
completion_criteria: Pydantic field descriptions contain only pure schema documentation.
All LLM prompt engineering instructions are successfully stripped from the models.
